title: noccaea caerulescens subsp. occitanica
intro: Noccaea caerulescens subsp. occitanica, also known as blue mountain rock cress, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make herbal preparations, including teas and tinctures, which have been associated with various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting antioxidant activity in the body. By consuming Noccaea caerulescens subsp. occitanica, individuals may experience relief from respiratory issues, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being.
